frameworks.name: Bayes factor
frameworks.one_line: ratio of marginal likelihoods used to quantify evidence between competing hypotheses

frameworks.name: significance testing
frameworks.one_line: frequentist p-value framework lacking interface with belief

frameworks.name: confidence intervals
frameworks.one_line: frequentist interval estimate disconnected from credibility of claims

frameworks.name: Bayesian philosophy of evidence
frameworks.one_line: evidence as that which rationally changes credibility of a claim

core_question: How should statistical evidence for competing claims be conceptualized and quantified?
key_assumptions: evidence must change the credibility of a claim in a reasonable mind
quantifying evidence requires an interface with degrees of belief
competing hypotheses can be assigned marginal likelihoods
key_findings: common frequentist statistics have no interface with belief and unclear evidential meaning
the Bayes factor provides a coherent quantification of statistical evidence
philosophical issues attend the use of the Bayes factor in practice
interpretations: statistical evidence is best framed via the Bayes factor between competing claims
use of Bayes factors carries philosophical commitments that must be acknowledged
limitations_noted: philosophical issues inherent in Bayes factor use
